investigation_summary: F4-FIND-001 was based on the Fase 1 dataset field git_tracked=True for .env. Direct verification in this phase shows: (1) git ls-files -- .env returns 0 entries in both repos; (2) git log --all --diff-filter=A -- .env returns no commits (never committed); (3) .gitignore in Project_ARIS already contains exact rule '.env' (line 1) plus '*.env' (line 6); (4) git check-ignore -v .env confirms active ignore rule. The git_tracked=True in the Fase 1 dataset was a false positive from the scanner — .env was on disk but not git-tracked. No git rm --cached action was needed or executed.
git_rm_cached_rationale: Not needed — .env was not tracked in any repo at time of verification
rotation_rationale: Even though .env is not git-tracked, the file exists on disk with 861 bytes of secrets. If .env was ever briefly tracked or shared by other means, rotation is still prudent. Operator must assess exposure risk and rotate as appropriate.
false_positive_explanation: The Fase 1 dataset (fase1_triagem_clasificacao.json, generated 2026-06-26 from commit a1f433f4d285850bcd628c1bc5f619f85f78ef33) recorded git_tracked=True for .env. This was a scanner false positive: .env exists on disk but has never appeared in git history (verified with git log --all --diff-filter=A -- .env, which returned no entries). This confirms F4-FIND-004 concern: the Fase 1 generator has reliability issues beyond quoting.
aris_active_context_mutation_scope: Governance artifacts only: ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json, ACTIVE_CONTEXT_SCHEMA.json, mirrors, DECISION_LOCKS.md, artifacts/lapidarium/
